How It Works

Your Onboarding Journey

Starting with FollowCare is simple. Here's what to expect from your first contact to your first support session.

1

Contact Us

Reach out by phone, email, or our contact form. We'll have a free, no-obligation chat to understand your needs and confirm NDIS eligibility. Interpreter support is available.

2

Your Support Plan

We work with you to develop a personalised Support Plan that reflects your NDIS goals, preferences, and any specific needs — cultural, language, medical, or otherwise.

3

Sign Your Agreement

We'll walk you through the Service Agreement and Consent Form at your own pace. You can involve a family member, carer, or advocate. No pressure — questions are welcome.

4

Meet Your Worker

We introduce you to your support worker — matched to your cultural preferences and language where possible. Your first session includes a detailed handover and orientation.

5

Ongoing Support

Regular check-ins and reviews ensure your supports are always working for you. You can adjust, change, or end services at any time — you are always in control.

Participant Documents

Your Documents — Download & Keep

These documents belong to you. Download them, read them at your own pace, and contact us if you have any questions.

Participant Handbook

Required Reading Key Document

Your complete guide to FollowCare — who we are, your rights, how services work, complaints process, medication assistance, cultural safety, and all key contacts. Read this first.

Download Handbook (PDF)

Participant Handbook Easy to Read

Easy Read Accessible Format

A simplified, picture-supported version of the Participant Handbook — designed for easier reading and understanding. Perfect if you prefer a visual format or want a quick overview.

Download Easy Read Handbook (PDF)

Need documents in a different format or language? Contact us at info@followcareservices.com or +61 431 116 739 and we'll arrange printed copies, large print, or translation into your preferred language at no cost to you.

Your Rights

You Have Rights — We Uphold Them

The NDIS Practice Standards guarantee you these rights. FollowCare is committed to upholding every one of them.

Dignity & Respect

Be treated with respect, fairness, and compassion at all times — regardless of background, culture, or language.

Choice & Control

You decide your supports, your goals, and your workers. Change or refuse services at any time — no questions asked.

Privacy & Confidentiality

Your personal information is protected and handled in line with the Privacy Act 1988 (Cth). We never sell your data.

Safe & Quality Services

All workers hold valid NDIS Worker Screening clearances. Services are delivered to the highest standards.

Speak Up Freely

Make a complaint at any time without fear. Your services will never be reduced or changed because you raised a concern.

Advocacy Access

You can have an advocate, family member, or support person with you at any time — meetings, signings, or complaints.

We Support Every Background

FollowCare is committed to delivering culturally safe services to participants from all walks of life across South Australia.

If English is not your first language, or if you have specific cultural or communication needs, we will work with you to ensure your supports are delivered in a way that is accessible, respectful, and comfortable for you.

🌏 All Backgrounds 🗣 Interpreter Services ✓ CALD Friendly
Cultural Safety

Services That Respect
Who You Are

FollowCare is proud to serve participants from Culturally and Linguistically Diverse (CALD) communities across South Australia. We understand that cultural safety is not optional — it's essential to quality care.

  • Professional interpreters arranged at no cost to you
  • Key documents available in accessible formats and languages on request
  • Workers matched to your cultural preferences where possible
  • Religious and dietary needs respected and incorporated into your Support Plan
  • Zero discrimination based on culture, language, or background
  • Culturally sensitive intake and onboarding process
Feedback & Complaints

We Want to Hear From You

Complaints help us improve. Every concern is taken seriously and handled fairly, promptly, and confidentially.

Our 5-Step Complaints Process

1

We Acknowledge

Your complaint is acknowledged within 2 business days of receipt — verbally, in writing, or via email.

2

We Assess

A designated Complaints Officer reviews your concern and identifies any immediate actions required.

3

We Investigate

Relevant information is gathered, all parties are consulted where appropriate, and confidentiality is maintained.

4

We Respond

You receive a clear outcome within 14 business days, with an explanation and any actions taken.

5

We Improve

Lessons learned are documented and used to improve our services for all participants.
